recap:
- Guardrails are essential for securing large language models (LLMs) against prompt engineering attacks by users.
- Implementing comprehensive guardrails is challenging due to the vast number of potential cases that need to be addressed.
- Guardrails can consume input token space, which may restrict the complexity of user prompts in single-LLM systems.
- Experimentation with prompt hacking and guardrails is encouraged in the AI Playground to understand their effectiveness.
- Continuous refinement and testing of guardrails are necessary to enhance the security of GenAI systems.
